canonical_definition: Based on the provided data, Constructiveness is a construct defined in the context of feedback generation. The single available definition characterizes it as the degree to which feedback identifies actionable weaknesses or areas for improvement that can help authors revise a paper. This concept is further described as involving "specific, critical feedback points targeting substantive weaknesses and improvement areas" ("Benchmark Profiling: Mechanistic Diagnosis ofLLMBenchmarks"). The construct is discussed in the context of "actionable feedback comments generation." The provided materials do not specify how this construct is operationalized or measured, as no measurement instruments or related behaviors are mentioned. Similarly, the data does not describe any relationships between Constructiveness and other constructs.
papers: Fair or Framed? Political Bias in News Articles Generated byLLMs
Improving Large Language Models Function Calling and Interpretability via Guided-Structured Templates
